name: Warfarin
text: Warfarin is a Anticoagulant metabolized via CYP2C9,CYP3A4 with a half-life of 40.0 hours. Known drug interactions include: Aspirin, Fluconazole, Clarithromycin, Metronidazole, Amiodarone. Contraindications: Active bleeding, Severe liver disease, Pregnancy. Adverse events: Bleeding, Bruising, Hemorrhagic stroke.

name: Aspirin
text: Aspirin is a NSAID/Antiplatelet metabolized via CYP2C9 with a half-life of 6.0 hours. Known drug interactions include: Warfarin, Ibuprofen, Clopidogrel, Methotrexate, ACE Inhibitors. Contraindications: Peptic ulcer, Bleeding disorders, Children with viral infection. Adverse events: GI bleeding, Tinnitus, Reye syndrome.

name: Metformin
text: Metformin is a Biguanide metabolized via Renal with a half-life of 6.2 hours. Known drug interactions include: Alcohol, Contrast dye, Cimetidine, Furosemide. Contraindications: Renal impairment, Metabolic acidosis, Severe liver disease. Adverse events: Lactic acidosis, Diarrhea, Nausea, Vitamin B12 deficiency.

name: Simvastatin
text: Simvastatin is a Statin metabolized via CYP3A4 with a half-life of 3.0 hours. Known drug interactions include: Clarithromycin, Itraconazole, Amiodarone, Grapefruit juice, Cyclosporine. Contraindications: Active liver disease, Pregnancy, Breastfeeding. Adverse events: Rhabdomyolysis, Myopathy, Hepatotoxicity, Muscle pain.

name: Clarithromycin
text: Clarithromycin is a Macrolide antibiotic metabolized via CYP3A4 with a half-life of 5.0 hours. Known drug interactions include: Simvastatin, Warfarin, Carbamazepine, Digoxin, Colchicine. Contraindications: QT prolongation, Severe hepatic impairment. Adverse events: QT prolongation, Hepatotoxicity, C. difficile colitis.

name: Fluconazole
text: Fluconazole is a Azole antifungal metabolized via CYP2C9,CYP3A4 with a half-life of 30.0 hours. Known drug interactions include: Warfarin, Phenytoin, Rifampin, Tacrolimus, Clopidogrel. Contraindications: Severe liver disease. Adverse events: Hepatotoxicity, QT prolongation, Stevens-Johnson syndrome.

name: Amiodarone
text: Amiodarone is a Antiarrhythmic metabolized via CYP3A4,CYP2C8 with a half-life of 1200.0 hours. Known drug interactions include: Warfarin, Simvastatin, Digoxin, Phenytoin, Cyclosporine. Contraindications: Severe sinus node disease, Second/third degree heart block. Adverse events: Pulmonary toxicity, Thyroid dysfunction, Hepatotoxicity, Corneal deposits.

name: Metoprolol
text: Metoprolol is a Beta blocker metabolized via CYP2D6 with a half-life of 5.0 hours. Known drug interactions include: Verapamil, Clonidine, Fluoxetine, Paroxetine, Rifampin. Contraindications: Severe bradycardia, Decompensated heart failure, Cardiogenic shock. Adverse events: Bradycardia, Hypotension, Fatigue, Depression.

name: Lisinopril
text: Lisinopril is a ACE inhibitor metabolized via Renal with a half-life of 12.0 hours. Known drug interactions include: Potassium supplements, Spironolactone, NSAIDs, Lithium. Contraindications: Pregnancy, Angioedema history, Bilateral renal artery stenosis. Adverse events: Angioedema, Hyperkalemia, Dry cough, Renal impairment.

name: Omeprazole
text: Omeprazole is a Proton pump inhibitor metabolized via CYP2C19,CYP3A4 with a half-life of 1.0 hours. Known drug interactions include: Clopidogrel, Methotrexate, Tacrolimus, Digoxin. Contraindications: Hypersensitivity to PPIs. Adverse events: C. difficile infection, Hypomagnesemia, Bone fractures, Vitamin B12 deficiency.

name: Clopidogrel
text: Clopidogrel is a Antiplatelet metabolized via CYP2C19 with a half-life of 6.0 hours. Known drug interactions include: Omeprazole, Aspirin, Warfarin, Fluconazole, NSAIDs. Contraindications: Active bleeding, Severe liver disease. Adverse events: Bleeding, TTP, Neutropenia.

name: Digoxin
text: Digoxin is a Cardiac glycoside metabolized via P-glycoprotein with a half-life of 39.0 hours. Known drug interactions include: Amiodarone, Verapamil, Clarithromycin, Quinidine. Contraindications: Ventricular fibrillation, Hypokalemia. Adverse events: Digitalis toxicity, Arrhythmias, Visual disturbances, Nausea.

name: Phenytoin
text: Phenytoin is a Anticonvulsant metabolized via CYP2C9,CYP2C19 with a half-life of 22.0 hours. Known drug interactions include: Fluconazole, Amiodarone, Carbamazepine, Valproic acid. Contraindications: Sinus bradycardia, SA block, Adams-Stokes syndrome. Adverse events: Gingival hyperplasia, Nystagmus, Ataxia, SJS/TEN.

name: Methotrexate
text: Methotrexate is a Antimetabolite metabolized via Renal with a half-life of 8.0 hours. Known drug interactions include: NSAIDs, Trimethoprim, Omeprazole, Penicillins. Contraindications: Pregnancy, Severe renal impairment, Severe hepatic impairment. Adverse events: Myelosuppression, Hepatotoxicity, Pneumonitis, Mucositis.

name: Fluoxetine
text: Fluoxetine is a SSRI metabolized via CYP2D6 with a half-life of 72.0 hours. Known drug interactions include: MAOIs, Tramadol, Metoprolol, Tamoxifen, Linezolid. Contraindications: MAOIs within 14 days, Pimozide use. Adverse events: Serotonin syndrome, Suicidal ideation, QT prolongation, Hyponatremia.

name: Ibuprofen
text: Ibuprofen is a NSAID metabolized via CYP2C9 with a half-life of 2.0 hours. Known drug interactions include: Aspirin, Warfarin, Lithium, Methotrexate, ACE inhibitors. Contraindications: Active GI bleeding, Severe renal impairment, Coronary bypass surgery. Adverse events: GI ulceration, Renal toxicity, Cardiovascular events.

name: Amlodipine
text: Amlodipine is a Calcium channel blocker metabolized via CYP3A4 with a half-life of 40.0 hours. Known drug interactions include: Simvastatin, Cyclosporine, CYP3A4 inhibitors. Contraindications: Severe aortic stenosis, Cardiogenic shock. Adverse events: Peripheral edema, Dizziness, Flushing, Palpitations.

name: Tramadol
text: Tramadol is a Opioid analgesic metabolized via CYP2D6,CYP3A4 with a half-life of 6.3 hours. Known drug interactions include: SSRIs, MAOIs, Carbamazepine, Ondansetron. Contraindications: MAOIs within 14 days, Uncontrolled epilepsy, Acute intoxication. Adverse events: Seizures, Serotonin syndrome, Respiratory depression, Dependence.

name: Spironolactone
text: Spironolactone is a Aldosterone antagonist metabolized via Hepatic with a half-life of 1.4 hours. Known drug interactions include: ACE inhibitors, Potassium supplements, NSAIDs, Digoxin, Lithium. Contraindications: Hyperkalemia, Addison's disease, Severe renal impairment. Adverse events: Hyperkalemia, Gynecomastia, Menstrual irregularities.

name: Carbamazepine
text: Carbamazepine is a Anticonvulsant metabolized via CYP3A4 with a half-life of 16.0 hours. Known drug interactions include: Clarithromycin, Phenytoin, Valproic acid, Warfarin, Oral contraceptives. Contraindications: Bone marrow depression, MAOIs within 14 days. Adverse events: Aplastic anemia, SJS/TEN, Hepatotoxicity, Hyponatremia.
